stem: A decision-maker must choose among 3 crop selection options. The probability of favorable weather patterns is uncertain, estimated between 39% and 58%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: rice: $68K in good weather patterns, $34K in bad weather patterns. wheat: $292K in good weather patterns, $62K in bad weather patterns. soybeans: $53K in good weather patterns, $21K in bad weather patterns. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.48: best option is wheat with EV=$172.4K. wheat is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [151.7, 195.4].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
wheat is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
